evals.name: weak-slogan
evals.prompt: Audit our current marketing vision: 'We will be the most loved brand in fintech.' Company context: B2B payments SaaS for mid-market companies, 200 employees, $30M ARR. We want to know if it's a real vision or just a slogan.
expected_assertions: Should score this LOW on all 3 axes (total < 4) and verdict = Slogan
Should call out 'most loved' as vague (Clarity penalty)
Should call out missing customer-truth link (Alignment penalty)
Should call out lack of decision-guidance (Actionability penalty)
Should produce a revised vision that names the B2B payments customer + a specific strategic capability
Should NOT produce generic 'visions should be inspiring' advice

evals.name: strong-vision-validation
evals.prompt: We're considering this marketing vision for our consumer hospitality marketplace: 'Reinvent travel by creating authentic local experiences.' Is it strategic enough?
expected_assertions: Should score this HIGH (total >= 7) and verdict = Strategic vision OR Worth revising
Should recognize the customer-truth link (authentic experiences)
Should recognize action-implications (kill generic-hotel campaigns, scale local-host content)
Should NOT mistakenly downgrade for being short — short is fine if specific
Should suggest only minor refinements if any

evals.name: no-vision-blank-slate
evals.prompt: We're a B2B HR-tech startup, Series B, 80 employees, $12M ARR, focused on mid-market companies struggling with employee onboarding for remote teams. We have no marketing vision yet. Help me write one.
expected_assertions: Should NOT skip the rubric — must score the proposed vision after generating it
Should fill in the vision-canvas template (Mission Anchor, Customer Truth, Strategic Capability)
Should produce a vision statement that names the customer (mid-market remote-team HR leaders) and the capability (e.g., onboarding-as-engagement-driver)
Should pass the 3 stress-tests OR explicitly flag which tests it might fail
Should NOT generate a generic HR-tech slogan ('empower modern teams', 'unlock talent')
